reviews.text: I must say, for a non vegan open to trying items, everything I tried was as good as it could be for know it was a bait and switch. The cuban pork and deli turkey were my favorites. Amazing selection of on site made vegan foods that totally replicate all your favourite prepared deli meats. Workers were very knowledgeable and friendly. 100% recommend!
This is an absolute must try! I’m not vegan, but I absolutely love the food here. Made from simple ingredients, the food and sauces taste amazing.

I had their fried chicken at the State Fair this year and it was amazing! I got a real meal at the fair which made me so happy. The texture and flavors were so delicious.

I went to the fair twice and the second time I had to get more of the chicken - it was my #1 favorite food item there this year.

Even my partner who isn't vegan and is very hard to impress with vegan food thought it was really good.

Been following this place on social media for years, but never got a chance to try out their products. Walked in the shop today and was amazed by how beautiful and authentic this place looks, really great job to the owners and team. Ordered two hot sandwiches, both were outstanding. The prices are pleasantly moderate and the servings are large. Five stars all the way, keep it up!
Ordered some items for shipping to another state. So glad I did! Arrived in good packaging to keep it cold. The pastrami, roast beef, and pepperjack were my favorite. 100% will order again.

Steak sandwich was burned beyond acceptable crisp. Tasted like ash. Can’t believe they brought it out to a table in such condition.

Burger was served wet with sauce. A little dab will do ya. Wife struggled to eat the burger.

Wings were amazing!

Why did I pay 15% automatic gratuities for such poor service and very badly burnt food?

We were so excited to try it out being a vegan place. Only 3 stars for food. Although they were kind enough to refund my overcooked BLT due to excessive oil/fat, my stomach couldn't take it and I lost my passion to eat there. We ordered Chicken sandwich, Steak sandwich, Mels ranch wrap, Wings and BLT. Except BLT and Steak sandwich other food were ok. For the cost of food, I was expecting something better. The staff were very courteous and willing to take feedback.
Parking is a bit challenging.

I always love a plant based restaurant! The chicken bacon ranch wrap was perfect and would be great with some extra sauce for dipping. We were not as big a fan of the mac n cheese. We got the frostbites, which are a thicker version of a milkshake. I wasn’t such a fan of these either, they tasted a little chemically to me.

The Service, Food is great. My new go to place. I ordered the chili Califlower wings, their very good. The location is very clean. The parking isn't the best, but it's not their fault. I loved being there. Nice music too. Check Them out.

I'm seriously so proud to live in a neighborhood where I can walk 5 minutes to a restaurant like j Selby's .The atmosphere felt "cool", windows covering the walls and lighter wood furniture leaving the dining space feeling bright, open, and comfortable. The staff were smiling, busy working, yet still approachable.
I was hit extremely hard recently in regards to health, and was out of work without pay. Today was the first day in a long while where I've actually worried about a meal, and remembered the community bowl offered by J Selby's. I felt like a criminal ordering it by phone, however it was an easy process. I wasn't expecting much as it is a free meal, however I was so incredibly surprised by how filling and well rounded it was. It was so delicious and packed with flavor. I'm not a vegetarian, but I know that I'll be back to try their other foods and will be donating to the community fund as soon as I am able. Thanks for taking care of Rondo y'all, this was so special.

I ate here yesterday and it was so good. It was only my second time having Ethiopian food, but I am obsessed with the injera bread. It’s so fluffy and light and and complemented the dish that I got so well. They also have items for sale that you can purchase if you want to make some dishes at home. I love that the menu is all plant based. I went with my boyfriend who is not Vegan and he got the spaghetti and really liked it. Will definitely be back!

This restaurant is all vegan. And the food is very good. I tried Sambusa appetizer which is good, next Awaze tibs which I normally don't see in another Ethiopian restaurant, i took the risk to try it and its ok for my tastebuds. I tried the baklava here as its rare to find a vegan baklava. They are all good

Lulu's was amazing, and really appreciate it's all VEGAN. 🌱

Owner's son greeted me & took my order; helped me narrow down my options, as there really is so much to choose from, even helped me figure out how to order smaller version of certain menu items, so I could try more dishes.

Lulu herself brought out my main dish, and she was so sweet & kind.

Everything came out fast, yet everything was super fresh, super hot & full of flavor. 🔥

Super impressed! It was my first time, but I will definitely be back. Thank you, Lulu & fam! 💜
Awesome intimate little place that serves unique Ethiopian food. The owner and her family are super nice and fun to talk to. I was glad to support their business in these tough times.

Super cute spot! Parking lot access via the alley. You can use the back door entrance.

My first time having Ethiopian food. Nice and clean inside. Love the colors and design inside.

I enjoyed our meal and the owner and family are very kind.

This was a great vegan place!  I don't often search out vegan. I have no issue with veg/vegan, and a friend is hardcore vegan; so, I took them there when they were in town.

This is a classic Twin Cities eclectic specialty restaurant /cafe soany of us renused to on the different neighborhoods!
Went for lunch and the mock duck Korean BBQ tacos were outstanding.  So much texture and the flavor of the (mock) duck was delicious; so,  you don't even think about 'mock meat'.  I want to note the fresh pickle were very good and great counter flavor pairing. I mean really good.  The arugula side salad was perfectly dressed and a good pairing with the crunchy sweetness of the tacos.  The BLT was good as well
They have a very good almost all regional microbrews.  But only pint pours (no half pours, I asked 😉)
Counter service and they bring out to based on a table flag.  It can be a little slow during peak lunch period, but it was worth the wait.  Some small table seating but shared long tables make up about half the seating area.  Also a counter area/bar area.
Counter can help you with any questions.  Very knowledgeable.

OMG the Caprice sandwich is divine!  The dressed greens make a WONDERFUL topping on it. JUST TRY IT!!! The potato salad has artichoke hearts in it & the sauce is definitely delicious! 😋
Ps. The potato wedges & aioli are a perfect substitute if they're out of potato salad.

Heavenly dishes…wow!!
The beignets were excellent and the Mac and cheese mature and Smokey. The lemonade was delicious. Great atmosphere, cool staff, happy it’s all vegan!!

Bright, airy vibe. Hopping busy, but got a seat easily and food was quick. Had the vegan eggs Benedict on a biscuit. Fantastic flavor and texture!

Pros: All vegan, Friendly staff, Great food

just outrageous ! - seriously gourmet dining - shouldn't have to be labeled "vegan", it's impeccable flavor profiles = fine dining experience + great business concept ... we enjoyed the mac & cheese / Cuban / Memphis Ckn...can't wait to get back & try all the other entree options - PS - worthy selection of quality beverages !

I recently ate at French Meadow Cafe & Bluestem Bar and had a really enjoyable meal. They have a large number of vegan options, which makes it a great spot for anyone looking for plant based choices.

I tried their dark chocolate sea salt cookie, which was rich and flavorful without being overly sweet. I also ordered a side of fruit, which was fresh and a nice complement to the meal. For my entree, I had a creamy mushroom pasta. I don’t remember the exact name, but it was very good and comforting, with a nice balance of creaminess and flavor. Overall, everything was well prepared, and I would definitely come back to try more from their menu.

Tried out French Meadow for the first time! Lots of options for breakfast along with the pastry and cake selections.

Corn pancake - shareable for 2, add butter and put the pure maple syrup on it. It elevates the flavor of the pancake!

Eggs Benedict - peppery but delicious hollandaise sauce and the hash browns were crisp on the outside and soft on the inside

Been here several times before. There are several locations, and this one on Lyndale is close to lots of retail shops and restaurants. There are plenty of seating options, including patio seating outside, indoor seating by the coffee area, or a more formal sit-down dining area with servers.

Usually there is a line to place an order, but this time it was quiet. To order, they have a large wall-mounted menu board, or hard copy menus you can study at your table. The pastry case on the left as you approach the cashier is filled with croissants, muffins, scones, and other baked goods, with plenty of amazing choices. To the right are the more formal desserts, including cakes, tortes, and tarts.

I ordered a London Fog Tea Latte (little lavender, little vanilla, extra foam). Normally I would get it in a mug, but we had to dash off. The barista did a nice job with steeping the tea and creating the extra foam. Next time we will stay for breakfast.

A very nice place for breakfast, big or small.

I have been to this establishment a few times now. It not disappoint. Classy, if modern, atmosphere. A decently expansive menu.

Good place to bring a date or just have a good lunch before running errands.

Had a delightful dinner here after a show at the Orpheum, the options were fresh, allergy friendly, and the service was professional and kind. Beautiful atmosphere, delicious food. Their vegan chocolate rose cake was such a treat!

Food was pretty good, good pricing too. Atmosphere is cool. Wish they accepted cards or Venmo, cash app, PayPal, ANYTHING. I took a bus for an hour here because this place was recommended to me and I didn’t have cash. I ended up approaching another customer and asking if I could Venmo her for cash. Thankfully she accepted.

Reminds me of the best of 90s era veg restaurants. Great value, quantity and quality. Had the Seiten Philly sub vegan cheese. Fantastic!

Write your own order and pay up front then find a seat and enjoy the vibe until your name is called.

5 stars for uniqueness of the ordering system, but 0 stars for efficiency and service of that system. You are basically responsible to taking your own order, but not through an app or a QR code: you must write it on a piece of paper that the person working the register (who was apparently extremely stressed out by their job) then read, gave you a total, and took your cash (only, no card) while simultaneously making any drinks you ordered. The person who rang us up had to restart my London fog, and made both it and my friend's chai latte with whole milk instead of the oat milk we asked for (she is lactose intolerant, just my preference). The food was okay. There were carrots in the gravy which was weird, but fine. Everything was both too salty and otherwise unseasoned.

Rad vegetarian and vegan spot with loads of character! We ordered dinner and started eating our delicious vegan baked🍪goods while we waited.
We ordered a Ruhben and Philly both Vegan. Both sandwiches were outstanding!!! We will definitely be back. Thanks for being totally groovy Hard Times Cafe🙏💚

It’s been surprisingly hard for me to find good vegan food in Minneapolis, but Hard Times is a gem. The vegan options are incredible, with tons of variety, all at prices that feel almost unreal by today’s standards, so I don’t mind it being cash only (there’s an ATM available if you need). We visit Minneapolis every year and this is a must-stop every single time. The vegan biscuits and gravy are my favorite, and all the baked goods I’ve had have been outstanding. Beyond the food, it’s very clear that the staff and restaurant are committed to creating a safe, welcoming space rooted in love and inclusion—while making it equally clear that hate (ICE) and harmful ideologies are absolutely not welcome. I love spending my money at a place that aligns so strongly with my values :) Can’t recommend it enough.

Love Seward cafe overall. Good prices, good ethics. I have especially enjoyed their vegan breakfast sausage and biscuits and gravy. I didn’t love the hash browns, too oily for me, but the tofu scramble was good. As far as drinks go, I have had mixed experiences. The chai was good one time but the next time it had an overpowering taste of cloves and I struggled to drink it (and I do like spicy chai) so I hesitate to order that again.

They have 2 single-user bathrooms which are both gender neutral.

Seward Cafe is a gem for anyone looking for amazing vegan options! I had the tofu scramble, and the smoky tempeh in it was hands-down my favorite part—so flavorful and satisfying. The fluffy pancake was also a standout, especially drizzled with maple syrup. Cozy atmosphere, great food, and friendly service. Definitely worth a visit!

The Seward Cafe holds a special place in my heart. I am very intentional when I go there, it's my "me" spot, where I go to journal and sit with myself. The grungy vibe is comforting, I really love this cafe.

They used to keep a carafe of coffee on the bar for those who ordered the Bottomless Coffee, that way I would not have to wait in line for a refill. Wish they would consider this again.

As someone who is GF, if wish there was a GF bread option available.

This has always been a favorite place because they have vegan biscuits and gravy on the menu. It’s been about 8 years since I visited and I’m happy to report it hasn’t changed much.

The quantity of food for the price is pretty much unmatched for vegan options. I ordered the biscuit breakfast with tofu. I was very happy with the cold press coffee, it was perfect.

Garden seating was so nice on an August morning. All of the space made it felt very private and chill.

I’ve been going here since I was a kid. I am so glad they reopened. I am also happy that they have the same menu. I ordered the super earth and it tasted just as I remembered it.
I know some people are a bit put off by the atmosphere, but I love it. It’s so eclectic and cozy. They have an awesome patio too.
It’s always been an order at the counter place. They call your name when your order is ready. They no longer have the order sheets to fill out.
I definitely recommend if you like good food with tons of vegetarian and vegan options.

Love at first bite is no joke. I’ll be dreaming of this burger for all of eternity. Brought my vegan mom here while in town and have to say it is the best vegan food I’ve eaten. Cannot wait to come back in the future!!! Meat eaters, you’ll have no idea it’s meatless.

This place is AMAZING!! The atmosphere was fun, the workers were so kind and friendly, and the food was 10/10. I have a gluten allergy and was pleasantly surprised that I could have almost everything on the menu!! They even have a separate fryer and space to prepare gf items, such a rare find and much appreciated! I will definitely be back and can’t wait to try more of the menu!

Never been here before, didn't know it was vegan but gave it a try. So glad I did, it was fantastic! Daughter got a Juicy Lucy and I got the Garlic Girl both delicious and juicy. Bartender Carlos was so kind and helped us decide what to get. I'll be back for sure.

8/30/2025 My boyfriend and I ate here for the first time tonight. 3 different employees checked in on us, one being the manager, and they were all very kind and very professional. The manager hung out for a minute and told us the story on the place which is really cool.

We got 3 food items; Garlic Girl, and BBQ Chicken, and chopped fries. We got 2 milkshakes; chocolate and lavender. Both came with whipping cream and a cherry. The food tasted sinful. It was so, so extremely good. I hardly had to use ketchup because it was so good. The food was served incredibly promptly and the portions exceeded my expectations. We had to get a to-go box.

The environment was very warm and welcoming and also clean, including the bathrooms.

I am so happy we went here tonight!!! We will be back!

We took a vegan sibling out for lunch. I was skeptical at first,but there were so many choices.  Our server was very helpful and answered our questions. This place by far has the best fries ever!!! The Garlic Girl and the Chai shake were drool worthy and delicious.  The atmosphere was very chill and relaxed. Everything was great and it definitely opens your eyes to vegan and plant based foods!! If you want a great plant based burger and amazing fries, this is the spot to go!!

the best breakfast in the whole twin cities ❤️ perfect options for those who are vegan or vegetarian! the chakra khan has been my favorite for a long time, but their good morning healing earth is also a favorite :-) thank you guys for everything you do for the community!! worth the wait every time!

Great vegan food - both the rice dish and burger were outstanding, and the cold brew coffee was great as well. It was great to see vegan options for nearly every dish on the menu. Definitely worth checking out if you’re looking for meat-free dishes!

Been going to Modern Times for years now and have always had great service from amazing people. If you haven’t gone, I highly recommend. It is a staple of the cities that I bring those from near and far to. Great veggie and vegan options while balancing with the classic eggs and bacon for breakfast. Im a fan of the south side hash, my wife is a fan of the rancheros and my brother loves the good morning healing earth. A place for all to come together over some good food and coffee. No wonder the community is so strong when they got a place like this in it to come together.

Great food and great vibes! It's a super cozy breakfast spot! I ordered the pumpkin pancake with a side of eggs and pancakes, as well as a chai latte. Everything arrived very quickly and was absolutely amazing. I loved the pumpkin pancake! This restaurant has plenty of seating, and the walls are covered in art created by children that you can buy, which I thought was very adorable. I would definitely return!

Absolutely wonderful and magical place! The service was great and the food was delicious! We loved how easy it was to make the dishes vegan. We had the southside hash and breakfast burrito, both were super delicious! We also got some donuts to take home and they were amazing too! Loved the atmosphere too!! Can't wait to be back!

Bole Ethiopian Cuisine has some of the best food, service, and atmosphere of any of the restaurants I have been to in the twin cities. We ordered the Tokkumaa and Yebeg Alicha to share and we're absolutely amazed and shocked at the amount, presentation, an quality of the food. Each bite was unique and delicious, filled with so much flavor and spice. We left completely full, in spirit and in stomach, several take out containers filled with food for several more meals later this week. Thank you Bole for the excellent service and community.

If I had to recommend 1 unique restaurant to visit in Minneapolis, this would definitely be it. Everything is so fresh and packed full of flavor. Ive been here a few times, tried multiple plates, and every one of them has been incredible. Highly recommend!

A friend recommended this place, and I'm so glad she did! We came here with a larger party of 9 people. They were able to seat us no problem and virtually no wait. Our waiter was great, informative, and checked in with us often. Despite being a larger party, we didn't have to wait too long to receive our food. The food was SO good. By the end of the meal, all of us were stuffed! And there was still food leftover. The food is also reasonably priced and not bad at all if you want to share dishes with your friends. The only and biggest downside was the heat in the restaurant. We went on a hot day in the evening, and the temperature was hotter inside the restaurant than it was outside. Most of us were sweating the whole time, and the heat was really hard to ignore after a while. Overall, I'd still say it was worth it, though.

Great flavors and options for a lovely Ethiopian meal. Our large group shared several plates and everything was delicious. Service was a bit slow and we were waiting on water refills or the chance to order now drinks a few times. Our group of seven were seated in a small booth with deep seats that felt a little cramped and warm. Overall, a nice dinner with a few small issues.

Such a wonderful experience! The food was incredible, and the staff was genuinely kind and welcoming, even while clearly understaffed that night and very busy. Our waitress never made us feel rushed and was excited to answer our questions, which made our first Ethiopian dining experience even more special. The pricing was perfect for the amount of food we received. We’re already looking forward to coming back!

Too spicy. I called in my order. I asked for "zero" spice, but they don't have that option, and didn't warn me. They made my veggie and chicken fried rice "mild", but both were too spicy. Very disappointing, and expensive. $42 including tax and tip. Smelled great though!

Everest On Grand will always have a special place in my heart. 16 years ago, when I was in mild labor with our oldest son, my husband and I ordered food from this restaurant.

Tomorrow Leo turns 16 years old. So on the eve of his birthday, out of nostalgia, we ordered the exact same dishes we ate before he was born.

Chicken Curry (Kukhura-ko Maasu), Goat Curry (Khasiko Maasu), Tomato Achaar and Potato Achaar.

It’s so lovely that this wonderful family-owned restaurant still exists, and we can share the exact same delicious dishes with Leo and his two other siblings.

We always eat here and it’s one of our favorite places in the twin cities. It has the best food, the service is really great, and we have honestly not found better in this genre of food in over a decade. Eat here, bring your family, and come hungry.  Fantastic place

My wife and I went here many years ago and had a fantastic meal. We always thought we would go back, but never did...until recently and wow, what did we miss...Great Food!  We went for dinner last month and lunch today. I ordered the veggie momos, veggie curry and roti. My wife ordered the chicken curry. Spice level perfect and I ordered it hot. So good! Our meal came steaming from the kitchen, prepared perfectly, and simply real home cooking.  This is a gem of a restaurant waiting for more people to discover. They have been around for a long time and their food does not disappoint. I am going back in a few weeks with a friend. Highly recommended.

Classic Nepalese/Indian decor; the samosas are huge, vegetarian & vegan entrees. Highly recommend their naan bread. Everything made in house and you can taste the freshness. Will be back. Perfect portions for two people to share.
really great vegan and vegetarian options, and some of the best spinach paneer i've had!! the staff is also always very friendly. always been worth the wait! :-)

Service was very slow.  We were seated then waited much to long for the order to be taken.  Only half full restaurant. Not once did the server come by to ask how is the food.
Also, the food took an extraordinarily long time to come out.  Was about 20 minutes.
Ordered the bone in goat.  Flavor was ok but far too many bones.  More than half the serving was bone.  I’ve had this other places and there should be some bones but more meat than just half.
Maybe another night or different server would have been better.

This has been one of my favorite places for years. The staff have always been wonderful and kind. They go above and beyond service wise. Making sure we had plenty of rice and even some extra raita with my to go order on the way out. Just the most thoughtful touches. They actually make their food fresh so yes, it may take awhile but I don't mind waiting. During my travels in other countries you often wait an hour for them to cook your food. Waiting for amazing food is definitely never an issue for me. 🤣 Highly recommend the creamy masala chicken, Golgappa, cauliflower peas and potatoes. Really anything on the menu you cannot go wrong with. Definitely a must visit place. ❤️

Worst . The only reason it has got this rating is because of the people who haven't tasted real indian food. I don’t know why people rated this much . The person who served had so much attitude . The rice was completely uncooked , chats are so less quantity and they were so cold and directly they added frozen vegetables to it . Aloo paratha was with 10percent aloo and dough, where dough was not cooked at all. We don’t ask for any refund nothing but they were not even ready to listen and showed lot of attitude to us . Worst experience ever.

Best food in Minneapolis, first time here and I truly loved everything I ate here .  We were a large group so we tried so many dishes and loved everything, the mimosas and coconut chai tea were also delicious.

My friend who visited from out of town specifically wanted to visit here because of the thukpa, which I thought was very tasty as well, upon trying it a bit. The dishes that I ordered, I found them to be mediocre to good overall, which is why I am giving this establishment a 4 rather than a 5 rating. Regardless, I know that they serve authentic Nepali food, so I would still recommend them to people, especially visitors.

The momos were pretty good in a conventional way with the sauce included, and it was a pleasant experience consuming them. They weren't out of this world, which is not a bad thing here. I personally think maybe having 3 or 4 samosas would work better as an appetizer, and the potato and pea fillings were a bit plain, meaning underseasoned.

Instead of getting goat curry as usual, I got the lamb curry this time, and it tasted pretty good in a conventional way as well. The lamb flavor along with the seasonings were there, along with the bones. Having rice with it was necessary. I thought having the extra diced tomatoes on top of the tomato curry sauce was pretty unique, but that could be because of my ignorance.

As mentioned previously, I would definitely recommend this establishment to those that are looking for authentic Nepali food. It's definitely a neighborhood gem in Minneapolis, even if I think there are areas of improvement.
10/10!!!! Amazing food, large vegetarian options, cozy atmosphere. They serve delicious garlic naan, flavorful curry, palak paneer & many more. I’m very much impressed!

My husband and I had a splendid lunch date at the Himalayan Restaurant. The seating outdoors is so beautiful and is decorated with many flowers. The atmosphere inside for lunch was quite peaceful, which is my ideal type of restaurant.
We ordered dumplings as an appetizer. My husband enjoyed the extra spicy sauce. I liked the sauce with tomatoes and spices.
The main courses are made to the spice level you prefer. The chicken tikka masala and the mixed curry were absolutely delicious. The naan was seasoned well with garlic and herbs. The white and brown rice were fluffy and cooked perfectly as well.
We are hoping to eat here again soon. Highly recommend!
Thank you!
This was very good Nepali food. Very authentic and everyone was super nice! Highly recommend coming here when in the Twin Cities. :)

This is one of my favorite restaurants. Great food and lots of vegetarian options. I never miss to order momos, garlic naan, and kataar aaloo made with jackfruit. Portions are good too! The owner of this restaurant is very friendly and always stops by to ask if everything was good. Definitely a great place to try Nepalese cuisine.

Amazing find! The food was SO good!!! Bonus, many options for people that are gluten free, vegetarian, or vegan. The cheese curds are the best I have ever had and I can’t believe they are gluten free. We wanted to try everything and ate WAY too much. My favorites were the cheese curds, the cauliflower steak with saffron aioli and the soup of the day which was tomato coconut bisque. The soup was so good that I was sad when I had eaten it all. My kids were so excited that they could get gluten free vegan cookie ice cream sandwiches. They were huge and one could easily be split with two people... especially if you order as much food as we did. My entire family loved the food! I wish we lived local because we would eat here all the time. I can’t recommend this place enough.

Celiac friendly fried AND fresh food. It’s a genuine treat to be able to have a place to order from for comfort food and pick me ups. The cheese curds with honey make a fun dessert. I wish I’d choosen the harissa sauce because I like things a little spicy!

Foxy Falafel has a great menu with simple choices that provide a variety of options and flavors without a lot of stress. The dishes are full of flavor, with three options for sauces and various combinations of meat and dish type. The prices are reasonable for the quality, and service is quick and has been positive in my experience. Overall, this is a great place for a lunch or quick get together.

Foxy Falafel serves up fresh, wholesome Mediterranean-inspired dishes in a cozy and causal space. The falafel is crisp and flavorful, and can be ordered in 3 flavors; the “Foxy” with traditional herbs, curry, and beet. We also ordered the baba ghanoush, which was amply portioned and had a rich, smoky flavor, and the marinated beets which were fresh and vibrant. The friendly service, ample indoor and outdoor seating, and the reasonable prices make Foxy Falafel an easy weeknight go-to spot.
Insanely delicious cheese curds (with honey on the side—so delicious!) and great food. The pita was a little dry and thin, but everything else was so awesome.

We had been wanting to try this place for years but somehow never got around to it until now. Maybe our expectations were too high? We are very adventurous eaters, travel a lot, and love big flavors. The service was excellent, and the atmosphere was nice, but the wall art seemed sloppy and amateur. I get it, it's supposed to be silly and wacky to match the theme of the restaurant, but it just didn't appeal to us.

Our drinks were good, but nothing to write home about. We had the tiger  prawn teaser, which tasted like a regular fried shrimp with no standout flavors. The "just shroomin" custard had a great flavor but was more of a mush/goop, and I understand it can't be a real custard because it's vegan, but the texture was unappealing.
The pork buns were good, but again, nothing standout or unique. The best dish by far was the market fish. Cooked to a perfect, blackened crust and with a delicious sauce. I could have used 3x the sauce. The potatoes that came with it were also good, but I would have preferred a different veg side that would, IMO, pair better with the fish. Overall we really liked it and would recommend it to anyone looking for a nice dinner date.

My partner and I went here to celebrate our anniversary. The staff made it very special for us! Everyone was absolutely wonderful. The food was incredible. We got the date night special, and each item was better than the next. My particular favorite was the gnocchi and the pork belly buns. The cocktails were to die for, I could have had nothing but the Erroneous Negroni-ous all night long 😅
This place is special and I'm not surprised it's been around as long as it has. cant wait to come back! Thank you tounge in check team for the very special experience ❤️

This place was a very unique and amazing experience. So many different flavors for the palate. From the starters to the dessert, each plate was unique and better than the last. One of the most memorable dining experiences and varied offerings for a relatively small menu that I’ve encountered in my myriad journeys across this planet. Best vegasm I ever had! (Ask your server for details ; )

We go out to eat often. Probably too often. It takes a lot to truly stand out at this point, and this place absolutely did.

Every bite was delicious. A special shout-out to the teasers, which we almost skipped because we weren’t totally sure what they were from the menu. They’re small, perfectly crafted bites and honestly one of the highlights of our meal. Don’t sleep on them.

The cocktails were incredibly balanced, creative without being weird for the sake of it, and the flight option is a dangerously delightful idea. The atmosphere is casual, cozy, and genuinely welcoming, the kind of place that immediately makes you want to settle in and stay awhile. Food-wise, the pad thai salad was a fresh, satisfying take on a classic. The jerk cauliflower hit the ideal balance of spice and flavor.

Service was fantastic across the board. Our waiter was knowledgeable and gave helpful guidance. Also, every staff member who passed our table checked in and made sure we had what we needed. A small but much-appreciated touch was fresh silverware and plates between courses. It’s the kind of detail that quietly tells you they care.

This place is an absolute gem in East St. Paul. A true standout and one we won’t soon forget. I’m already looking forward to going back.

Everything was excellent! The cocktail flight was creative and delicious, the pad Thai was full of flavor, and the smashed potatoes were perfectly crispy. The pork belly buns were melt-in-your-mouth good. Great food, great service — can’t wait to go back!

My dad and I have been here twice now.  Both times, our food was absolutely delicious.  I took takeout for my partner and he loved his food as well.
They have a wide variety of options and most dishes can be made with your preferred protein.  Their level 5 could be a little hotter for me, but it is still delicious!! The flavors are wonderful.

It's been a long time since I've been back to the restaurant after covid 19. The restaurant used to be very crowded. Recently, my husband and I went on the weekend and the restaurant wasn't very crowded. The food was very delicious. The price was reasonable. The waiters were very friendly and cheerful. The restaurant was very clean. I will come back more often.

I had an amazing meal yesterday. FYI - The restaurant does have a service charge. If you don’t like it too sweet, you can request for no sugar or less sweet. Our meal was amazing last night.

Shrimp & chicken spring rolls 8/10 It was delicious.

Sai Oua 10/10 It’s a Thai herbal sausage. You can taste all the spices and herbal such as lemongrass, ginger, galangal etc. sausage was not dry. It was great!

Papaya salad (Lao style) 10/10 so good, so delicious. The fish paste that was in it is not too strong. I like it less tamarind and this was good!

Crying tiger 10/10 the steak was cooked to a medium perfectly. The dipping sauce that comes with it was delicious.

Pricing is definitely on the higher side, but for the portion you get, the taste, service was really great. I always come back and recommend people to come eat here.

The service is great, and the atmosphere was very nice. The Khao Soi had a good flavor but the portion was very small. I ordered their Crying Tiger which is a classic authentic Thai dish, and unfortunately I was VERY disappointed!  The meat was grisely/tough, and the sauce was too sweet and thick, when it should have been a spicy, tangy lime sauce to go with tender slices of beef.
I'll give it one more chance, because I will have to try the Tom Kha soup, to see if they can redeem themselves....I hope so!

worse Fried rice ever. I got a Thai style fried rice that only had onions and bokchoy . Didn’t taste anything like a fried rice . No flavor at all not even salt. Would not recommend this place to anyone.

Love the outside of the store with its pretty pink facade. We found this place after visiting Children's Minneapolis for a dr visit. We were welcomed so warmly as soon as we walked in and enjoyed the vast array of unique donut flavors offered including vegan options. You can tell that the bakery and staff are very supportive of the city which is something that always makes me happy. We hemmed and hawed over a couple flavors because there were so many good options that it was tough to decide. The lovely lady that was helping us made sure that was not a problem and gave us two free donuts on top of the half dozen we ended up paying for. We loved all of the options we brought home but especially the bananaramma, the caramel apple cheesecake and the chocolate w/ salted caramel. We'll be back again in the future without a question.

My first time coming I was refered by a friend since going vegan and had a taste for a donut; I got two of each of 4 vegan donuts selections (Vanilla Starlet, orange, ginger & cinnamon infused glaze, Sugar Kane (vanilla glaze), and a vegan strawberry), so 8 donuts total. The flavors and sweetness are there, however it was dry and hard almost like eating dry old bread. I came around 4pm the store sign said closed at 6pm so I may have came at the old batch time but I tried and doubt I go back.

The atmosphere of this place is certainly worth the visit alone. It's a great experience from start to finish and I couldn't seem to look away from the 50s interior paired with the vintage items, but I was captivated by the paintings.
The donuts had a ton of options, with a bunch of really fun flavors. I had to go with a classic to kinda get a good feel for the place before I got a fancy flavor. So strawberry it was! Which the donut itself was awesome, loved the croissant texture to it.
The one thing I would say about this place is the donuts are really rich. The toppings remind me more of frosting than of glaze. I hope to be back, but next time I'll certainly be grabbing a coffee to even put the sweet.

Other than that the staff seemed friendly, and there were lots of vegan options. So I would recommend this place.

My First Time Ordering Through Glam Doll Donuts In Minneapolis MN With DoorDash
I Got Some Half Dozen Assorted Dark Angel
Femme Fatale,Misfit And Chocolate Starlet And My Drink Was Strawberry Malt Shake. Definitely Will Come Back Again To Glam Doll Donuts.

First time back to MN in more than 10 years, STILL amazing!
I didn't think to ask her name (She made the model donut shop in the glass box on the wall), but our server was friendly and awesome. Had a Pecan Scotchie, a Calendar Girl, half of my wife's Showgirl, and hot chocolate. EPIC! Go! Eat donuts!

This is one of the more low-key vegan spots in Minneapolis, but don't overlook it! It comes off as very healthy, which doesn't always pique my appetite, but Seed has never steered me wrong! I absolutely love everything I've had from them! Their kale Caesar wrap has kale and tempeh, two ingredients that I don't always like, but I think about that wrap so often! The ancient grain granola at brunch was a similar story. I'd also recommend the tahini chocolate chip cookie and chai latte. Its location along the Green Way is superb. It's a great stop on a bike ride!

At first I thought, a cafe, and a yoga studio, in a strip mall for lunch?
It is now one of my favorite places for a light flavorful lunch time. The food here is very fresh and clean without being bland healthy. I had the chicken salad (plant based). It comes with potato chips (haha). It was so fresh and delicious you would never know it wasn't the real thing. I've had it 3 times and never a fault. They also have what I think is the best cookies in the Twin Cities. They sprinkle them with sea salt, and it just elevates the flavor. I've only really seen that on cooking shows but, here you can live the experience. It's relaxing and clean, with many tables inside and outside. I noticed many come also for the smoothies. It's a happy and mostly healthy place for sure. The people are also very nice and helpful here. It also doesn't have that frantic restaurant vibe, it's nice to relax and eat your lunch in peace. Check it out.

Cute plant-based food spot. Came from breakfast. The berry French toast was good, the tofu-take on 'scrambled eggs' wasn't good but worth a shot. The teas choices were great and tasty. Love any place trying to be plant based so gave an extra star, although the food was only 4/5. 🐨

I have only had the best experiences here! The Legacy Wrap is the BEST wrap I've ever had. It's wonderful to go somewhere completely vegan. The bakery items are always super yummy. The vibe is very peaceful but bright and cheery.

After following them for months on Instagram, Seed Cafe is officially open next door to Moda Yoga studio across from Whole Foods by Bde Maka Ska.

Everyone in the restaurant were so nice and helpful. The cashier suggested the Kale Ceasar Salad and Chickpea Toast.
I was blown away at the how the Chickpea Toast mimicked a tuna sandwich. It was soooooooo good. I mean really really good. Great job on creating a stellar sandwich.

The Kale Ceasar was a bit overpriced for a salad but the dressing, capers and home made croutons gave it a nice touch.

There were so many other items on the menu that seemed as if they would be just as tasty.

Looking forward to trying a few more next time.

One of my favorite spots to hit up when I visit the Cities. Those croissants are DELICIOUS! The bodega is expending every time I go with amazing vegan snacks I’ve never seen anywhere else in Minnesota. Check their Instagram as the menu changes often, and they are great about updating what they have throughout the day as stuff can sell out quick.
Monday dinner? Yes , Please! Vegan & yummy. I enjoyed this food prepared by Chef Mitchell today of Healing Foods Minneapolis. Thank you for the wonderful meal and fresh-pressed juice!

Great food, great conversation great prices. Always organic, progressive, supporting local vegan startups.

Go and check it out for vegan food, gifts, groceries and intelligence.

Progressive supporting and lifting marginalized communities as well as animals and the environment

I absolutely love this spot and the owner. This year marks my 2nd year attending the "Pay What You Can" ThanksVegan Dinner.

When I arrived, there was a short line of folks waiting patiently as the owner Colin scooped up plate after plate. Always reminding us that he was doing his best to complete the orders. He cooked all of the food and served it as well.

Eureka is a small bodega located in St Paul approximately 25 minutes from my home in Maple Grove. You can find all types of plant based items in the bodega to purchase as well as daily menu specials.

Pay what you can Vegan Dinner at Eureka Compass hit the spot. The owner is a blessing for doing this. The food is definitely made with love.

Menu:
👉🏾Cornbread
👉🏾Greens
👉🏾Herbed Potatoes and Carrots
👉🏾Garbanzo and Root Veggie Fritter
👉🏾Squash and Black Bean Casserole
👉🏾Pumpkin Scone

Colin, the owner of Eureka Compass Vegan Foods, is the sort of person who goes out of their way to serve his customer base. His gluten free scones are amazing and never boring or tasteless. He is responsive to customer feedback and requests for different flavors etc. Thank you so much fro bringing such an amazing business to the Hamline Midway.

First time in this little shop. Atmosphere is super cute and friendly. The staff was amazing and I believe their entire menu is vegan! Huge plus for me :). I tried the vegan vanilla ice cream which was really good, and the cocoa banana crepe. So much to say about this crepe! First of all, it was served quickly and so hot and fresh I had to wait 20 min for it to cool off enough! It holds heat very well 😂. The crepe was huge and served in a pizza shaped cardboard sleeve. Absolutely delicious. Plenty of filling and overall just a lot of crepe! Would absolutely recommend and will be back to try more menu items!

Employees were super patient and allowed me to sample several flavors before ultimately ordering a complex mix of flavors in a cone! I don’t remember the last time I had a waffle cone but this shop was entirely vegan so I went for it! We also tried a savory crepe (ham and cheese) and it was delicious!! Cold brew coffee was very reasonably priced. Indoor and outdoor seating along with bathrooms available. This place has it all! Stop in and you’ll see!

Amazing ice cream flavours and their cones are Fresh and crispy. I was delighted to try the rose water cardamom flavour and the wild berry flavours. They also do crepes - with plant based ham and steak and can make them vegan. I was really impressed with the quality and will definitely be back!!

Believe the hype: This is the best vegan ice cream in the metro and beyond. It’s made fresh from scratch right on site.

Whether you like the classics (vanilla, chocolate, strawberry) or you have a more adventurous inclination (rose water cardamom, earl gray bergamot) — your palette will be happy.

Be sure to try the crepes! This place does its savory as well as its sweet. We loved the cheeze steak and  ham-n-cheeze.

Love the plant based/vegan ice creams available here!  There are many creative and delicious flavors here!  There's an outdoor street patio during warmer months, but little seeding inside.  Parking is residential or next door in the semi-empty lot.
I ordered the: Strawberry-sorbet+ Basil + Avocado-lime + Rosewater-cardamom.  My love ordered the Ube & Cookie Monster flavors.
Check it out and happy eating!! 😀

Pizza Luce was highly recommended. I would say overall, it was okay. The Spinach Artichoke Dip was good, but a little on the salty side. We ordered the Classic Pizza and it was good. Loved the crust.

Our server was just meh. I asked for recommendations on a local beer and he asked which ones on the menu I wanted. He couldn’t give a good recommendation. He was also slow. We had to track him down to get our check and again to pay. He only had 2 tables of people to serve.

Phenomenal food does not begin to describe our experience. The Luce pizza was amazing. The winter special, a spicy pesto pasta, was delicious. The pretzel was fluffy. Mustard tasted like something was missing, but was still delicious.

The bartender taking care of us provided excellent service.

We are in town for 5 days and we will be making sure we visit at least once more while here.

What a great place to spend time eating delicious pizza. The atmosphere is fantastic and welcoming.

Our server was so kind and helpful. She said she’s been there for 12 years because it’s such a good place to work.

I was visiting from out of town, first time to Minneapolis. This place came recommended by the hotel staff and I am so grateful.

We ordered for the office today. I had the Ultimate Veggie vegan hoagie. Whomever made it needs to be checked. Toppings extremely sparse. Insanely huge pepper stem that could not have been missed when they made the sandwich.🤬 You knew full well that you were being funny when you made it! Just plain lazy. Not cool when the hoagie was already skinny, so it made for a VERY hard bite. Nowhere near the photo. Not that it had to be, but sheesh! At least put forth effort when you are making food for someone else when this is the job you chose. No, it was NOT busy. We placed the order right at 11:00 and it was delivered at 11:30 am, so do not say "workload." Dead wrong. Period. Plus, a colleague found onions on what was supposed to be just a cheese and pepperoni pizza. Someone clearly did not want to work today. Well, you should have stayed HOME. Definitely did not deserve the tip I know was paid. Pictures have been included.

The Pizza was tasty. Custom flavors available and the staff was friendly and knowledgeable.
Wiley was fantastic,  so helpful and made the experience Taylor made to our tastes.
Highly recommend
And they are open late!

Delicious! The most hospitable and friendly staff. Excellent prices. Large servings. Fries are seasoned so uniquely and tbey are delicious. Also, they have the BEST most Moist and delicious baklava I have ever tasted! Hands down.

Wow!  I am a long time resort manager and restaurant creator, I am not easily impressed.  What I can tell is that the staff, management, and ownership, are Passionate about their food and restaurant operation.  The business seems to attract great people who truly Care about what they do and serve their customers.
Go to SHISH!  Quality, fresh ingredients, well seasoned, unique and prepared with pride.  Nice gluten free and vegetarian options, too.  We had a salad, Lamb Curry, and the Lamb Shank Special.
I don't live in MN, but I will visit anytime I am in town.

We enjoyed our meals.
Brought home slice of delicious turtle and raspberry cheesecakes.

Delicious tenderloin and chicken kebab. Forgot to take photos of the main meal

Shish was delicious!  I loved their crispy seasoned fries to go with the Leo Burger. 😋. The chicken shawarma atop the hummus really hit the spot, and the veggies were sweet and crisp! We wish our city had a Shish!

I love this place and have been coming here for over a decade. Great food, great atmosphere. However - prices keep rising! Today I ordered a gyro wrap (no rice, no fries, etc) and a coffee and paid over $15 - before the tip! This may be a "get off my lawn" moment, but, that seems excessive.

Bowls usually aren’t my thing, but my friend and I wanted to try something new for our monthly meetups. Boy, are we glad we did because it was absolutely delicious. I got the strawberry clouds and ate every last bit of it. No complaints here…I love supporting black owned businesses.
Great people and real juice. Great picture of Nipsey to go with my Nipsey Drip! Nice atmosphere. The bowl was so good! Go here!!

Love The Dripping Root! We tried the smoothie bowls and they were FANTASTIC. The flavor combinations go so well together. Some of the photos you see (including mine) can make it look like the bowls are small...but they are larger than they appear and are the perfect size in a 16 oz bowl. Service is welcoming and friendly, and ordering is easy and efficient! We loved the window seat and the stools here are so comfy which is really nice. Also, the shop is gorgeous and so clean! We can't wait to visit again to try the rotating smoothie bowl flavors and the juice that we've heard such great things about. Thank you Dripping Root!
Very filling smoothie bowls and even more to choose from as well . Got the summertime bowl it was delicious def will be back for more

The inside of this place is super cute! Tons of colors and plants. Friendly, helpful staff. The have clothes and other merch to take home too! When I went they brought back there summer juices so I got watermelon and strawberry, both were very yummy and fresh. I will be going back to try the smoothie bowls!

One of my favorite restaurants around campus if not my favorite... Best sweet potato fries in the world. Chicken fantastic (pictured) and chicken curry are highlights, somali steak sandwich is also great. Been here many times and it's incredibly consistent.

I was staying at the Graduate Hilton hotel that is very close to this restaurant during a trip to Minneapolis in July 2025. One evening, a couple of friends and I decided to have dinner there. When we went, there was a small crowd. We immediately got a table and ordered four dishes. The food came pretty soon and were plentiful. Each dish was very tasty. Also, it was unbelievably cheap. We were very happy with our meal and would gladly go back there.
Had the Steak dinner and a Sambusa (Could have eaten 3) Delicious!!! Absolutely amazing experience.

A cute and colorful spot, perfect for a quick bite! I ordered lamb gyros and French fries. I was served in five minutes and everything was hot and tasty! The pita is slightly toasted to give a little extra oomph to the gyro meat and the portion was very generous. French fries are crisp and only needed a little salt, though they did come with a spicy sauce to dip if you have stronger taste buds than my wimpy butt. Definitely worth trying more, since the baklava looks excellent.

I recently tried Afro Deli for the first time, I ordered the Gyro Jabati wrap. It costed around 13 dollars after taxes.

The service was good, however it took around 6-10 minutes to recieve the food. With the meal it came with fries but there was no drink included. (There is free water though)

The atmosphere was nice, seating is a little limited but the space they did have was excellent.

My problem however is with the food itself, I had high expectations for Afro Deli. Ive heard about their operations for around a decade now and expected the food to be Excellent. I am also very familiar with Somali Cuisine.

The fries were great but the wrap its self had a few key problems which greatly impacted the experience, and I am not a picky eater at all. Unfortunately, the wrap was overfilled with Tzatiki sauce which blinded all other aspects of the wrap, also the rice was dry.
The Jabati itself was great condition and the gyro meat was great too. The given sauce container which I think was a spicy mayo was good too.

Overall the food I got here was better than most places but it did not meet my expectations. Every other aspect was expected and tolerated.

Love this place!
The food and service was excellent!
I do wish they had more open hours, but then they are open they are a gem!
They are a sustainable restaurant and use produce from their own garden. The food doesn’t get fresher than that!
They have inside and outside dining.
They also have great coffee/espresso!

We love Common Roots Cafe, and had them cater for our wedding. The food was absolutely *delicious*. Everyone loved it, and I still think about that turkey at least once a week. They went above and beyond with the planning process leading up to the wedding, and were so incredibly helpful and thoughtful. I loved getting to work with them, and cannot stress how much I appreciated all they did for us!

I have dietary restrictions, and we selected passed apps for cocktail hour that I couldn't eat. They asked me ahead of time if I would like them to make a couple things special for me and I told them not worry about it as I'd have plenty of food shortly after for dinner time. They flagged me down during cocktail hour with a plate of specially made appetizers just for me. And I don't mean, like, a simple veggie tray or something. They specifically went out of their way to make a few gluten free and dairy free versions of the tarts we were serving guests, just for me. Unbelievable service and care.

Amazing food, and incredible employees. We highly recommend both their catering and their cafe!

We went here for weekend brunch. The food was great! My only complaint is that it’s outdoor seating and there could have been a couple of umbrellas. We did get a nice tan. :) the lavender latte is my fav!

Good times were had. Service was great. As part of my first time out to MN, I would say this place is easily worth the visit.

When done with meal, you bus yourself. Keep that in mind, they don't spoil you here with service of that level, but instead exchange it for delicious food and tasty coffee.

Pictured: Shakshuka, Small Hot Mocha, Large Hot Salted Caramel

Great local coffee shop with delicious food options! They have unique latte flavors - I tried the salted caramel which was delicious! Pictured is the broccoli and tomato frittata and the farmers breakfast- both of which were excellent! They also have delicious gluten free bread. There’s a good amount of seating and a nice outdoor patio!

Incredibly sweet staff even though we ordered takeout in the last hour they were open. Meatballs and lamb kebab were incredible, as was the hummus and pita. But the chutney is really where it’s at. Mainline that into my soul.

It's been a while, just getting around to write a review on our incredible experience here. Staff were extremely helpful & friendly. Authentic menu and everything we had was delicious. I'd recommend Khyber Pass Cafe for any occasion but it'll be a fun date night out.

Delicious meals, with great homemade Chai.  I appreciated​ the warm flatbreads but the chutney could have been a little better.  I loved my chicken kebab, the sauce on top was exquisite.  A little pricy.
Food and service was outstanding. Small and quaint restaurant in a great location and free parking lot close by.
Beautiful restaurant, yummy food. Take note they’re currently to go only. And not open for dine in. Highly recommend

I had a very disappointing experience at this restaurant. One of the staff members—a young woman with a nose ring—was quite unfriendly throughout the process. She kept giving me unpleasant looks while preparing my order, and after finishing my bowl, she slammed the mixing bowl down onto the floor. To make matters worse, the food itself wasn’t good. The black rice was barely edible. I wouldn’t recommend this place—best to avoid it if you can.
A lovely place. Very clean. Workers were very attentive and engaged. Lots of fresh options. Got the Caesar salad, and it was fantastic! Will need to come back and try the Verde Bowl sometime.
Employees were super nice and helped me find my new favorite meal. Would highly recommend to anyone!

This place is amazing! They had one of the best salads I ever had, (I ordered the crispy teriyaki), but everything my family ordered we loved! Superb design, very clean, great staff, very friendly manager too! Clean bathrooms, good location, I can’t even stress how amazing this place is!

Service was fast, but I just can't give more stars when it's order and pay at the assembly line. Food was fresh and the facility was clean. If there is a washroom here, it's hidden. Healthy options but has a fast, subway like vibe.
I love that they have Gluten Free options here and most of the time the gluten free options are actually palatable, whereas some gluten free stuff is nasty as hell.
Excellent service and good food :) lovely atmosphere for lunch, coffee, or a chat.

Selection for groceries has improved. Staff and food are always lovely and I can buy almost all of my favorite groceries.
The little deli/cafe is good. I've had several things made for me that I just didn't like even though i had liked them other times. There can be mild quality fluctuation.  I recommend keeping it simple--the ingredients themselves are typically very,  VERY good anyway.

It's a nice place. It's part grocery store/deli/bakery but also a meeting/community space. I am pretty sure that you can't bring outside food but correct me if I'm wrong.

I like it because having casual community spaces is important, and it's great for meetups.

They have board games and two separate meeting rooms, one with a kitchen, in addition to general seating. Reservations for the meeting rooms might cost some cash.

Nice little place! I like they have a little bit of grocery selection as well as the food counter. Especially since I am not a co-op person and like really "normal"/plain food. I had to order off the kids menu at the food counter and they let me order a plain drink that isn't on the menu too. They don't have the same drinks like at Seward. But I liked playing Pinochle (card game) with friends. Nice size seating area. And a little enclosed space they have classes in (they were having an art class while I was there which was fun to peak in on). I will be back for more cards ;)

First time coming here with some friends, not a ton of seating, but it was easy enough to get a table for three of us. The quiche was great it was fluffy and super cheesey and it came with with a large side salad. The almond croissant was amazing, nice and flaky and nutty. The breakfast burrito was just okay. The coffee drinks were all super tasty. Overall, I liked this place and will be back for the quiche!
Absolute banger of a spot. Great food, coffee, and culture. Love this staff!

Food was alright. The Bacon and Swiss quiche was relatively good. Didn’t really like how they didn’t tell me that it came with a salad, which I don’t like. Atmosphere is also pretty nice. Liked the paintings they had in there. Relatively convenient as it’s right along the route 14 bus line and a 6 minute walk to the 22 and 8 minute walk to the 23

Beats Starbucks by a huge mile. It's not just the coffee that is good and reasonably priced, but the food is also quite excellent. The croissants are freshly baked, some of the best I've had in the US. Also tried the pesto pizza with goat cheese, and it did not disappoint.

I cannot leave May Day alone; I'm always excited to come here. The staff are always calm and patient, the baked goods are always mind blowing and the coffee never disappoints. I feel like I can truly relax and decompress here, and always enjoy interacting with whoever's working.

As one of the few, if not the only place, that has nasi lemak, this establishment does a great job in providing dishes that range from Chinese to Malaysian to Singaporean cultures. I believe that since COVID they have transitioned to takeout only, and it's great to see that the business is still thriving even if they are still operating under the takeout only model despite how the world has evolved away from the pandemic.

For the few dishes that I came here for, qualities varied, with some dishes lacking flavor while others simply sloppily created. The Hainanese chicken relied solely on the extra dipping sauces for flavor while the chicken itself had no flavor alone. The nasi lemak had good flavor mainly because of the sambal, and the chicken had good texture. I didn't see any peanuts in the dish, which I thought was needed for nasi lemak. The sizzling beef and ginger scallion duck had pretty expected flavor because of their ingredients, meaning that what they looked like was what they tasted like, if that makes sense.

When I got food from there a second time 4 months later, overall the food was better. The chili tofu wasn't spicy at all even if I asked for spicy in my original order. The char kway teow had both good flavor and soft texture, allowing a positive tasting experience. The sambal shrimp had a good amount of vegetables and not enough shrimp, and had very good flavor because of the sambal infused with the dish.

Because their menu is huge, I think they have an adequate selection of options that can serve customers of all kinds. Even though this is not one of my favorite spots within the Twin Cities, the dishes they have already solidifies this establishment as a "must not miss" within the area.

I wish I had known about this place when dine in was an option. I lived in Malaysia for a few years and have tried several restaurants in different states that say they are Malay. However, it wasn’t the same. I had resolved to just find “American” Malay when someone told me of this place. To my surprise my favorite Malay food was available and amazing. Chicken Satay (Malay) is different than other satay and this place nailed it!  I also tried the Roti Canai (presented slightly different than stands in Malaysia) and the flavor brought me right back to Kuala Lumpar… ❤️. I will be ordering from them again!

Only takeout was available, which was a little disappointing since I’m not from Minneapolis and ended up eating in my car, LOL. The food itself wasn’t super authentic, but it still gave me a nice taste of home. The real highlight was the milk tea—it was really good and definitely worth recommending!

I ordered vegetable fried rice no eggs. Wow it had so much flavor! It was piping hot, No soy sauce needed! I also ordered one of the mango cream desserts and it was so good I wish I would have got another, the mango was ripe and sweet, perfect for my first try, will definitely be back. Thank you!

My first time trying indonesian/malaysian/singaporean food, and let me tell you, it was very good. I had Char Kwai teoy, chicken satay, roti cana, and pilut hitam, and it was really good. I am super impressed guys. You guys did good and I would most definitely eat this food again. Good job.

While visiting St. Paul to see the Minnesota Wild play and go to see Muse in concert, I happened upon Ngon Bistro.
From the moment I arrived the staff was absolutely friendly and incredible. After getting some local brew suggestions, I ordered the Chicken Pho. As a huge Pho fan, I have to say this probably is the best broth I have ever had! The chicken was absolutely incredible and portion size was right on point. I also had the wings which were incredible as well. With both things I highly recommend their house made hot sauce which was amazing!
Overall it’s one of the best dining experiences I have ever had. Ask for Junior, Zach, or Lisa as they were very friendly and very great at their job! Incredible service and incredible food with a low key vibe. Highly recommend this place!!

Update: patio is open. Dog friendly patio.

The pho is so amazing! There are fun drink options and all local beers served. The owner places great importance on keeping the menu fresh and new. Great quaint atmosphere.

It was okay.  I wasn't blown away with the food.  I got the bun bowl.   The noodles tasted like they were at least two days old.  The chicken tasted like it had been reheated in a microwave.  My friend got the pho burger.
It was decent but didn't really taste anything close to pho.  However, the service was exceptional and our waiter was knowledgeable and very friendly.

Driving down University Ave one Saturday afternoon, looking for a place to eat dinner, I passed by Ngon Bistro, and thought I'd give them a try, so glad I did!

The food here is Vietnamese/Asian fusion, and includes favorites like pho, spring rolls and bahn mi.  In the summer months there is a wonderful little outdoor patio for dining, but I decided to stay indoors.  The dining room is bright and cheery, thanks to light colors and huge picture windows at the front of the store.   It's fun to sit and watch the streetcars go by on the Metro's Green Line!  You can use the Victoria St. station stop to get here.

The menu is a simple one page affair, with a small but excellent selection of Vietnamese/Asian inspired food.  I was also given a page of daily specials.  Lots's of great food to choose from!   They also have a small selection of signature cocktails for you to try.  I had the Lemon Basil Negroni, and it was excellent!

For a "small plate" appetizer, I decided on pork egg rolls, and they were wonderful.  They came with a nice, sweet dipping sauce.  I had no problem eating them both!

My main course was the pho with meatballs.  Oh my goodness!  It was the best soup I've ever had in my life!   And so very filling.

The staff here are really great people, and wonderful to deal with.  The decor is simple, bright and cheery.  Parking can be an issue, but I had no problems finding a spot close by.  The food itself is OUTSTANDING.   Bar service is top notch.

If you stick to just a main course and water, you can dine for about $20 a person.   Add adult drinks and appetizers, and your looking at $50 a person.

I give Ngon Bistro my HIGHEST RECOMMENDATION!  I do plan on coming back again.

The food is absolutely stunning! We had the fresh spring rolls and goatwurst to start, both were unique and flavorful. Main dishes were a brisket and grits with ramen... they had a kick! The atmosphere is relaxed, yet the staff is attentive and friendly. 10/10 will return!

Honestly I have been here for the past 8-9 years including during the pandemic so I don't know why I have not given it a review yet, but suffice it to say that for someone like me who doesn't even really eat cake, I will come here specifically for cake. That's how good they are. Before and after the pandemic, their cake quality has stayed consistent. They also publish on their website what cakes they have everyday in addition to the 9 types that are available everyday, so that those that really enjoy a special item can plan ahead. I took photos of some of the items that I have traditionally ordered the most, both before and after the pandemic, but I would also recommend the classic vanilla cheesecake and the turtle cake, which are not pictured.

In addition to cake, their hot food is also pretty good, with various options of soup and bread available for a reasonable price and pizzas baked fresh next door near the register where one would order or pick up whole cakes. Prices have increased over the years and for some reason the pizza doesn't taste as fresh (both the basil and the mozzarella), and looks a bit smaller, which might be my illusion. That doesn't change the fact that this establishment is worth a 5 in my book, though. Whenever I have friends visiting from out of town and wants to try cake, this would be the first place I bring them to.

The chicken dumpling soup was delicious as well as the smoked turkey sandwich. The German chocolate cake was a bit too sweet for me but overall good. The service was outstanding, shout-out to the girl serving the soup who gave me the soup recommendation :)

One of my favourite spots for dessert date night. Have only tried the sweet treats so far, which tend to be on the pricier side. Have dined in a few times but depending on the time/day it can be very busy and hectic. The raspberry cream torte, banana cake, flourless chocolate cake, and vanilla tres leches were all very tasty.
One of my favorite dessert places of all time! The food is also phenomenal and it's always bumping in there. Its even better when they have live music. Highly recommend!
The best place for desserts! Their turtle cake is excellent, featuring rich chocolate layers, toasted pecans, and a smooth caramel drizzle that creates a delightful balance of flavors.

I forgot how truly delicious the food is at Brasa. I needed to pick up food for an event, and decided to get take out.

We started with the fried sweet plantains, really caramelized and yummy. They are served with an interesting green dipping sauce. Lovely. We had the pulled pork sandwich, which is served with pickles and green sauce. The meat was so tender, and the accomplishments really complemented the pork. I absolutely adored the smothered chicken sandwich, which is served with red sauce and coke slaw. My goodness - this was DELICIOUS! They both came with house made tortilla chips - these were just as yummy the next day!

I enjoyed the butterscotch pudding also. The only slightly negative note was the chocolate cake, which was dry despite the whipped cream and topping.

I heartily recommend Brasa, in particular that fabulous smothered chicken!!

You can eat in, or order for takeout also.

We had Brasa cater our wedding and they were absolutely incredible! From start to finish, they were efficient, easy to communicate with, and so accommodating to our needs. The food was absolutely amazing—our guests couldn’t stop talking about it. On top of that, they went above and beyond by leaving us a thoughtful to-go bag with food and champagne at the end of the night. It was the perfect touch after such a special day. Highly recommend Brasa for any wedding or event!

One of our family favorites whenever we go through Minneapolis! The food is always fantastic, authentic & fresh!!!
I always go with a BOWL! I recommend the smothered chicken (it's so moist) or the pork bowl! Everytime we go I buy a hot vinegar to take home as well. Brasa would easily be a 5 star place but the recent move to QR code orders is a bit frustrating.

Hidden gem! I came here for the 1st time last week for a staff meeting. Upon arriving, everyone was extrmelely friendly. We were seated immediately and given menus to review. I ordered the rotisserie chicken with Yuca fries, Mac and cheese, and cornbread. Everything arrived very quickly. I’ve been to numerous restaurants, and the wait time here was almost non-existent. The food came out within 5 to 7 minutes of ordering. The waiter was extremely attentive and continuously returned to see how we were doing and checked in to see how everything tasted. The rotisserie chicken I ordered with the green sauce was phenomenal. The mac and cheese and everything else was exceptional. I would definitely return. I had a really great experience and the environment was very welcoming.

What can one say about Brasa that is not already spoken for in just one meal with them?
The service is spectacular, servers are responsive and knowledgeable about the menu, and the cozy atmosphere allows for a fast transition from seating to eating.

Shoutout to Enrique for being super helpful in walking us through menu options!

The menu never disappoints. The piece of paper itself is neat and detailed. As a couple with annoying dietary needs, my lover and I are always grateful to be able to determine quickly which dishes are okay for us to eat, able to be substituted, or need to be avoided. From paper to palette the quality continues, with a delightful combination of South American, Soul, and Caribbean cooking.

Overall, one of my favourite restaurants in this city. Give it a try!!!

After working outside in the cold today my boss and I wanted to get something hot to eat.  I told him great spot.  The pho as usual is so good.  The large is enough for two meals so I’m excited to have it for dinner as well.  Quite easy to talk and no wait.  Easy to get some work done quick and warm up.  Can’t wait to come back

I was looking for a nice place to eat pho and was recommended IPho. I was very please with my visit overall. The food was VERY good. It came out very quicky and smelled delicious. The service was also very kind. Everyone there is respectful and attentive to customer. I will definitely go back!
Reasonable prices, great broth, and amazing bahn mi. Cold? Hungry? This is your comfort zone. Highly recommend adding broccoli to any pho dish.

Everything about this place is amazing. From the food to the service, we had such a great experience coming here. We were immediately seated and food was brought out within 20 minutes or less. Extremely friendly and professional staff. I look forward coming back!

The food here is absolutely amazing!!, they are very quick bringing out your food, Im talking about soon when you order your food is hot and ready in front of you within 5 minutes

The staff is also very friendly and attentive, never have to wait long to get served or noticed. I absolutely love going to this place when im craving pho as it gives you great proportions and fills you up for sure 💓

Very cool spot! Had great reviews so stopped in for dinner with the family. We had the cheesy bread with marinara which was good. We also ordered the grilled vegetables appetizer that we enjoyed.

Then my wife and daughter ordered a pizza with tomatoes, feta cheese, harissa and fennel sausage which they really enjoyed. My son is a basic pizza kid so we split a pepperoni pizza which we also enjoyed. We all liked the coal fired approach which reminded us of some of the places we enjoyed when we lived in CT (New Haven).

Service is going to get its own section here in the write up because it was exceptional! We had an Asian guy (apologies I can't recall his name) who took care of us who was fantastic! Positive attitude, super upbeat and friendly. Very helpful with menu suggestions and beer suggestions as well. Very attentive, refills were timely, never had any long stretches where he disappeared (and they were PACKED). Really stayed on top of everything and provided a great experience overall.

Excellent meal and experience and I highly recommend you give it a shot if you're in the area.

Service was prompt and tentative. Pizza came out and was cooked perfectly. Crispy bottom with just enough flop to it. Ordered a simple pepperoni. Sauce is light and refreshing. Cheese had very good flavor. Pepperoni were on the mild side but worked well with all the flavors. The crust had nice spots of char without being over cooked.
Always great food and service.  Love the crispy, bubbly crust. You won’t be disappointed with any pizza you choose.
Our server was amazing. We had 14 people on a Saturday and she was on top of it. Great food and loved it had Christmas decorations!

Attentive staff and excellent service. We got the hot salami pizza and it was good though not hot enough in my personal opinion. They have root beer on tap that is delicious and decent beer. We’ll be back to try more pizza!

Our waiter, Jake, was excellent, and all the wait staff assisted each other. The food was very flavorful and we'll presented. They made fresh decaf coffee for my wife, which she said was excellent. We went during happy hour, and highly recommend coming here if in the area.

First time visit since they refreshed the restaurant - it’s very nice.  They also refreshed the menu, better starters and more options than burgers.  Service was excellent. Very good vibe.  By the time we left, there were 15 people waiting outside to get in. Dog friendly patio.  Nice bar.  We had the shrimp tacos, guacamole dip, and chip & dip, all were good. The Expresso martini is very good. Their beer selection is unique and well crafted.

The hamburger experience was yummy and well cooked, in a great way!! Great music! Beers were great, they only carry local beers so keep that in mind. Truffle fries were good with a generous amount of parmesan the cheese dusted on them. The gouda hash was totally scrumptious! The tacos are to write home about!

This location has an elevated feel and modern design on the inside. Exemplary staff & dining experience. The burger special was a toast to the Alps with Raclette & perfectly caramelized onions. The beet salad never misses. I also tried one of the mocktails and it was very delicious with blood orange. Worth a stop or two or three.

I can't believe I'm rating this low. Especially because I love the food at the Uptown location so much. The whole restaurant is beautiful, much more aesthetically-pleasing than some of the other locations. The service is on par with the one other location I've been to. They're extremely attentive. The food, however, was just...off. We started out with the cheese curds and onion rings--both of them good. For the next part, we split a few dishes--a bowl of the tomato basil soup, the Ahi Crisps, and the 60/40 burger. The drizzle on the tuna was way too sweet and salty. It really needed something like citrus to brighten it up. The burger was a literal hockey puck. It was honestly worst than a burger from a church cookout. I don't remember being asked for the meat temp. Needless to say, it was not our normal medium-rare. It also had nothing on it. You just got a mouth full of hardened fat and umami. There was no dimension. It needed to be cooked less and have vegetables. The soup was so incredibly acidic and salty that I asked them to take it away. I ALWAYS FINISH MY FOOD. I ended up vomiting when I got home because I had such bad acid reflux, something I've only had one other time in my life. I'm not picky about food and I'll eat almost anything and enjoy it so this is really weird for me. I can only really speak for myself but several other people in my party said they had mal-effects immediately after dinner, as well. Unfortunately, we were only in town for the Vikings game so we won't be back. Someone did come around and asked if everything was okay.
